briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — finesse spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a minnow-profile trailer (like CrushCity Freeloader) for natural, erratic movement - it adds realism and bulk without impeding the natural balsa-like hunting action of the ChatterBait.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a minnow-profile trailer (like CrushCity Freeloader) for natural, erratic movement - it adds realism and bulk without impeding the natural balsa-like hunting action of the ChatterBait.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, ledge. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: For hooksets, reel steadily and let the rod load up before setting the hook instead of snapping the rod - this prevents the blade from popping open the bass's mouth and increases hook-to-land
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, ledge. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: On clear water grass lakes, most anglers fish the inside line where fish spawn. But fish also spawn in the deeper scattered grass patches (7ft+) in the middle of big flats. Nobody else fishes it — that's a competitive advantage. A lot of larg...
Yamamoto pro Hite: Standard Zako for ChatterBaits (subtle natural action), Kickin' Zako (paddle tail) for deep heavy ChatterBaits on ledges with poor light penetration and for swim jigs exclusively

Johnston: You have to be able to judge the size of bedding fish without catching them to make sure you're going to get the right ones. Spend time gathering waypoints on spawning areas before tournament day.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal spawning bass become extremely lockjaw but won't abandon beds. Fish are still in 2-5ft but much harder to trigger. Slow way down and make repeated presentations to visible beds.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
